evidence.scientific_clarity: 6-cluster KDE ridgeline with partial overlap and left-side labels exactly matches the brief; progressive expression trend is visually encoded.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; default DejaVu Sans used throughout; text sizes (9–11 pt) are acceptable but font choice is the matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: Palette includes both #55a868 (green) and #c44e52 (red) — a red-green pair that fails deuteranopia/protanopia colorblind safety; no CB-safe palette explicitly chosen.
evidence.layout_density: overlap=0.55 gives good ridge stacking, figsize=(7,5.5) is appropriate, spines cleaned, y-ticks removed, no label collisions observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes specific quantitative claims; however, non-selectable Type 3 fonts and the red-green palette would likely draw reviewer comments.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts reports Type 3 (DejaVu Sans) fonts — text is not selectable; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (44 KB), figure.png (74 KB), caption.tex (372 B), and gen_figure.py (2.3 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the method (Scott-bandwidth KDE), sample sizes (300–500 per cluster), and states a specific finding (progressive medians, Cluster 6 widest spread).

overall_strengths: Clean ridgeline construction: appropriate overlap, suppressed spines, color-coded left labels, and a well-sized figure.
Caption is publication-quality: method, sample sizes, and a concrete directional finding are all stated.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): PDF text is Type 3 and non-selectable, failing vector-fidelity requirements.
Red-green color pair (#c44e52 / #55a868) in the palette is not colorblind-safe; a perceptually uniform sequential or CB-safe categorical palette should be used.
